response.justification: The candidate brings a genuinely relevant blend of geospatial/GIS domain experience and low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching, high-performance databases handling millions of requests/second), plus some C and C++ exposure from NI LabWindows/CVI and a C++ wave-field-synthesis project. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) in a commercial setting, and the candidate's recent and primary work is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with C++ being older and peripheral. There is no Rust and no direct navigation/routing-pipeline experience. A screening interview is warranted to gauge depth and willingness to ramp on modern C++, but it is not a clear match.
strengths: Strong geospatial/GIS domain background (multiple GIS platforms and map-data projects)
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching, real-time data)
Some C/C++ experience plus systems-language depth in DLang and Linux familiarity
concerns: C++ experience is dated and peripheral; recent focus is TypeScript/Node.js, not modern C++
No Rust experience, the only acceptable alternative to C++
